title: Alignment system
abstract: An alignment system comprises a first diffractive optical element comprising an image and a laser source. The image is visible when the laser source is viewed from a desired field of view through the diffractive optical element. The laser source may be configured to produce a beam of light that will extend through at least a portion of the first diffractive optical element, thereby illuminating the image. The first diffractive optical element and the laser source may be disposed in a vehicle.
claims: Claims Claims: 1. An alignment system, comprising: a first diffractive optical element comprising an image; and a laser source; wherein the image is visible when the laser source is viewed from a desired field of view through the first diffractive optical element; and wherein the laser source is positioned to, upon activation, produce a beam of light that will extend through at least a portion of the first diffractive optical element. 2. The alignment system of claim 1, wherein the first diffractive optical element and the laser source are disposed in a pillar of a vehicle. 3. The alignment system of claim 2, wherein an aperture is disposed in an exterior wall of the pillar; and wherein the aperture in the pillar is aligned with the laser source and the first diffractive optical element so that the laser source is configured to send light through the first diffractive optical element and through the aperture. 4. The alignment system of claim 3, further comprising a selectively transparent cover; wherein the selectively transparent cover is disposed over and configured to cover the aperture. 5. The alignment system of claim 4, wherein the selectively transparent cover is an electro-optic element. 6. The alignment system of any of claims 1-5, wherein the laser source is configured to produce a beam of light that extends from the laser source through the first diffractive optical element in a generally perpendicular orientation as it passes through the first diffractive optical element. 7. The alignment system of any of claims 1-6, further comprising a second diffractive optical element; wherein second diffractive optical element is generally parallel to the first diffractive optical element; wherein the second diffractive optical element is disposed between the aperture and the first diffractive optical element; and wherein the laser source is configured to project a laser beam through the first and second diffractive optical elements at a generally perpendicular angle. 8. The alignment system of any of claims 1-7, wherein the alignment system is in communication with a biometric scanning apparatus. 9. The alignment system of any of claims 1-8, wherein the laser source is an eye-safe laser source. 10. The alignment system of any of claims 1-9, further comprising at least one mirror or focusing lens configured to direct light from the laser source toward the first diffractive optical element. 11. A scanning system comprising: a first diffractive optical element having an image that appears when light from a laser source is viewed through the first diffractive optical element from a desired field of view; a laser source configured to generate a beam of light and shine the beam of light through the first diffractive optical element; and at least one scanning apparatus disposed to capture images from the desired field of view. 12. The scanning system of claim 11, wherein the scanning apparatus is a biometric scanning apparatus. 13. The scanning system of claim 12, wherein the scanning apparatus is an iris scanner. 14. The scanning system of any of claims 12-13, wherein the scanning apparatus is a facial recognition scanner. 15. The scanning system any of claims 11-14, further comprising a second diffractive optical element disposed generally parallel to the first diffractive optical element. 16. The scanning system of any of claims 11-15, wherein the laser source is an eye-safe laser source. 17. The scanning system of any of claims 11-16, wherein the scanning system is disposed in a vehicle. 18. The scanning system of claim 17, wherein the scanning system is disposed behind an aperture in a pillar of a vehicle. 19. The scanning system of claim 18, further comprising a selectively transparent element; wherein the selectively transparent element is disposed within the pillar and is disposed to cover the aperture. 20. The scanning system of claim 19, wherein the selectively transparent element is an electro-optic device.
